title: %Error: Expecting expression to be constant, but can't determine constant for FUNCREF
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

```
module ring_connect #(
  parameter int unsigned   N_IDS           = 16,         // must be even, > 0
  parameter int unsigned   N_IDS_PER_STAGE = 2,          // must be > 0
  parameter type           ID_T            = logic[3:0],
  parameter ID_T           SRC_ID                        // must be < N_IDS
) (
  input  logic             clk
);

   localparam int unsigned N_STAGES = (N_IDS / 2) / N_IDS_PER_STAGE;

   typedef ID_T id_2d_arr_t [N_STAGES-1:0][N_IDS_PER_STAGE-1:0];

   function automatic id_2d_arr_t gen_stage_ids (bit reverse);
      for (int st=0; st < N_STAGES; st++) begin
for (int id=0; id < N_IDS_PER_STAGE; id++) begin
   int delta = ((st+1)*N_IDS_PER_STAGE) + id;
   if (!reverse) begin
      gen_stage_ids[st][id] = SRC_ID + delta < N_IDS ? SRC_ID + delta :
                                       SRC_ID + delta - N_IDS;
   end
   else begin
      gen_stage_ids[st][id] = SRC_ID >= delta ? SRC_ID - delta :
                                SRC_ID - delta + N_IDS;
   end
end
      end
   endfunction

   localparam id_2d_arr_t     STAGE_IDS = gen_stage_ids(0);
   localparam id_2d_arr_t REV_STAGE_IDS = gen_stage_ids(1);

   generate
      $info("%m: N_IDS=%0d N_IDS_PER_STAGE=%0d SRC_ID=%0d STAGE_IDS=%p, REV_STAGE_IDS=%p",
   N_IDS, N_IDS_PER_STAGE, SRC_ID, STAGE_IDS, REV_STAGE_IDS);
   endgenerate

endmodule

module tb_ring_connect;
   localparam int N_IDS           = 18;
   localparam int N_IDS_PER_STAGE = 2;
   localparam int ID_W            = 5;

   typedef logic [ID_W-1:0] id_t;

   localparam id_t SRC_ID = id_t'(3);

   logic    clk;

   ring_connect #(
      .N_IDS          ( N_IDS ),
      .N_IDS_PER_STAGE( N_IDS_PER_STAGE ),
      .ID_T           ( id_t ),
      .SRC_ID         ( SRC_ID )
   ) dut ( .* );

endmodule
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Error: test.sv:30:43: Expecting expression to be constant, but can't determine constant for FUNCREF 'gen_stage_ids'
                     : ... note: In instance 'tb_ring_connect.dut'
        test.sv:19:22: ... Location of non-constant ARRAYSEL: Array select LHS isn't simple variable
        test.sv:30:43: ... Called from 'gen_stage_ids()' with parameters:
           reverse = 1'h0
   30 |    localparam id_2d_arr_t     STAGE_IDS = gen_stage_ids(0);
      |                                           ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.041 for more assistance.
%Error: test.sv:31:43: Expecting expression to be constant, but can't determine constant for FUNCREF 'gen_stage_ids'
                     : ... note: In instance 'tb_ring_connect.dut'
        test.sv:23:22: ... Location of non-constant ARRAYSEL: Array select LHS isn't simple variable
        test.sv:31:43: ... Called from 'gen_stage_ids()' with parameters:
           reverse = 1'h1
   31 |    localparam id_2d_arr_t REV_STAGE_IDS = gen_stage_ids(1);
      |                                           ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

VCS:

```
vcs -q -full64 -sverilog test.sv

Note-[IEST] $info elaboration system task
msg: tb_ring_connect.dut: N_IDS=18 N_IDS_PER_STAGE=2 SRC_ID=3 STAGE_IDS='{'{'hc, 'hb}, '{'ha, 'h9}, '{'h8, 'h7}, '{'h6, 'h5}} , REV_STAGE_IDS='{'{'hc, 'hd}, '{'he, 'hf}, '{'h10, 'h11}, '{'h0, 'h1}}
location: file test.sv line 35
path: tb_ring_connect.dut                                      


1 module and 0 UDP read.
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
verilator --binary --sv -Wno-fatal test.sv
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

```
Verilator 5.041 devel rev v5.040-181-ga64774726
```

What OS and distribution are you using?

```
Linux 4.18.0-553.22.1.el8_10.x86_64
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
